DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT ALLSTATE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL LUIS SEGURA AND YOLANDA SEGURAS COMPLIANCE WITH COURT ORDER AND FOR AWARD OF MONETARY, EVIDENTIARY AND OR ISSUE SANCTIONS: THIS IS NOT A REQUEST FOR CONTEMPT AS ALLSTATE ARGUES IN REPLY. THIS IS A DISCOVERY MOTION. ALTHOUGH CCP SEC. 2030.030 PERMITS THE COURT TO TREAT A FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH A DISCOVERY ORDER AS A CONTEMPT OF COURT, TO DO SO, THE MOVING PARTY MUST COMPLY WITH CCP SEC. 1211, AND THE COURT MUST FOLLOW THE FULL CONTEMPT PROCESS AS REQUIRED BY THAT SECTION. THAT HAS NOT BEEN DONE HERE. THEREFORE, ALLSTATE'S MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO SEEKING THE REQUESTED RELIEF AT ANOTHER TIME BY ANOTHER PROCEDURE. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. Allstate Insurance Companys Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Luis Segura And Yolanda Seguras Compliance With Court Order And For Award Of Monetary, Evidentiary And Or Issue Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-02-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Demian Oksenendler for Plaintiff.
No other appearances.

THE PREVAILING PARTY STIPULATES TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PREVAILING PARTY AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ALLSTATE INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Compel Luis Segura And Yolanda Seguras Compliance With Court Order And For Award Of Monetary, Evidentiary And Or Issue Sanctions:

This is not a request for contempt as AllState argues in reply. This is a discovery motion. Although CCP Sec. 2030.030 permits the Court to treat a failure to comply with a discovery order as a contempt of court, to do so, the moving party must comply with CCP Sec. 1211, and the Court must follow the full contempt process as required by that section. That has not been done here.

As a discovery motion, it fails for two reasons.

As to the first, it is a basic tenet of the Discovery Act that in order to compel further responses to interrogatories, compel further responses to requests for admission, or compel a further response to an inspection demand, the movant must notice the respective motions "within 45 days of the service of the verified response, or any supplemental verified response, or on or before any specific later date to which the propounding party and the responding party have agreed in writing." Cal. Civ. Proc. Code Sec. 2030.300(c)
(interrogatories); see Cal. Civ. Proc. Code Sec. 2031.310(c) (demand for inspection of documents); Cal. Civ. Proc. Code Sec. 2033.290(c) (requests for admission). Failure to timely notice said motions results in waiver. Id. Sections 2030.300(c), 2031.310(c), 2033.290(c); see Sexton v. Superior Court, 58 Cal. App. 4th 1403, 1410 (1997) (holding "[f]ailure to timely move to compel . . . constitutes a waiver of any right to compel a further response; indeed, similar provisions have been held at least quasi-jurisdictional . . .[in] the sense that it renders the court without authority to rule on motions to compel other than to deny them").

Why Allstate failed to "re-notice" it's motion to compel until July 2020 is unclear. But what is clear is that if it is more than a re-notice, and itself is a new motion as it contains additional pleadings and materials, then that new motion is untimely and is therefore denied.

As to the second, if it is simply a "re-notice", then Allstate must rely on its original papers, which it does not dispute failed to contain a separate statement as required. Failure to file a separate statement gives the Court the authority to deny the original motion. See Cal. R. Ct. Rule 3.1345(a). Therefore, the original motion that is "re-noticed" is denied for failing to contain a separate statement.

But, the Court wants to be clear, the denial of the motion to compel is without prejudice to AllState's arguments, in limine or otherwise, that the discovery answers by Plaintiffs were deficient and subject to other relief such as evidentiary or issue sanctions at the appropriate time, or that Plaintiffs are in contempt for failing to comply with a Court order. The Court today makes no judgment one way or the other on those matters. Decisions on such requests, and whether they are timely, are saved for another day.

Therefore, Allstate's motion is denied without prejudice to seeking the requested relief at another time by another procedure.

The request for sanctions is denied.

order signed.
Judge Pro Tem: Jason Yurasek, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
